D3 Chemistry L1 Identify Reference

Nassau's 15 Causes of Colour

Kurt Nassau (1983) catalogued every physical mechanism that produces colour into fifteen distinct causes — from incandescence to band gaps to scattering — covering everything from a rainbow to a soap bubble

The Physics and Chemistry of Color (Nassau 1983, 2001) is the canonical taxonomy: every coloured object on Earth fits one (or more) of fifteen mechanisms. Causes 1-3 cover light emission (incandescence, gas excitation, vibrations); 4-7 cover transitions in molecules and ions; 8-10 cover the solid state; 11-15 cover physical optics (scattering, interference, diffraction).

Pick a cause from the list to see its name, one example, and the typical wavelength range.
